title: Chapter 13: The Broadcast
content: Friday. Day twenty-eight.
Chloe woke at 5:00 AM and did not move.

She lay in bed and listened to the sounds of the facility waking up. The buzz of the intercom system powering on. The distant clatter of the kitchen. The footsteps of orderlies beginning their rounds.
Today was the day.
At 6:00 AM, the intercom crackled.
"Good morning, patients. Breakfast will be served at 7:00. Recreational Period will begin at 11:00. Please proceed to your assigned activities."
The same message. Every morning. A recording.
Chloe sat up and pulled the phone from her sock.
Fully charged. The recording was saved. Ready.
She dressed slowly. Deliberately. Each movement felt ceremonial.
Breakfast was quiet. The women of Ward B knew something was happening. They could feel it in the air.
Mia sat across from Chloe. Amara sat two tables away, her phone hidden under her jacket.
"The file transfer is complete," Mia said quietly. "Priya confirmed. The lawyer has everything."
"When does the story break?"
"Tomorrow. The journalist needs twenty-four hours to verify."
"We cannot wait twenty-four hours."
"Why?"
"Because if we wait, they will find out we sent the files. And they will lock down everything."
Mia's fork stopped moving. "What are you proposing?"
Chloe looked at the speaker in the ceiling. The intercom system.
"How many speakers are there in this building?"
"Every room. Every hallway. Every common area. The system is centralized."
"Can you access it?"
Mia's eyes widened. "You want to broadcast your recording through the facility's intercom?"
"I want to broadcast it to every room. Every patient. Every staff member. Every camera."
"That is insane."
"It is effective."
Mia stared at her for a long moment. Then she smiled.
"I will need ten minutes to bypass the admin panel."
"You have five."
Five minutes.
Chloe stood in the hallway outside the admin wing. Mia was in the supply closet, tablet connected to the router, fingers moving across the screen.
The intercom speaker above Chloe's head was silent.
"Almost there," Mia whispered through the earbud. "I need to bypass three authentication layers."
"How many have you bypassed?"
"Two."
"How long for the third?"
"Thirty seconds."
Chloe looked down the hallway. Nurse Cross was at the far end, talking to an orderly. She had not noticed Chloe yet.
"You have twenty."
The third firewall fell.
"Access granted," Mia said. "The intercom system is yours. You are live in five seconds."
Chloe pulled the phone from her pocket. Opened the recording.
"Four. Three. Two."
She pressed play.
Her voice came through every speaker in Serenity Peak.
"My name is Chloe Hart. I am twenty-two years old. I am being held against my will at Serenity Peak Wellness Center in upstate New York."
In the dining hall, three hundred forks stopped moving.
In the admin office, Dr. Webber looked up from his desk.
In the third-floor private room, Amara Osei smiled.
"This facility is not a hospital. It is a corporate-run institution that profits from incarcerating women who refuse to conform."
Nurse Cross's face went white. She ran toward the admin office.
"Patients here are not diagnosed by doctors. They are referred by family members who pay forty thousand dollars per patient."
In Ward A, women emerged from their rooms, drawn by the sound.
In Ward B, Tiffany stood frozen, tears streaming down her face.
"The director, Dr. Marcus Webber, has been funneling profits to offshore accounts. My father's law firm manages the shell company that owns this facility."
Dr. Webber was shouting at someone. The intercom. Turn off the intercom. Now.
"I recorded this from the Isolation Wing. They put me there because I refused to stop telling the truth."
Chloe's voice paused. Then:
"But the truth does not stop just because you lock it in a room."
The recording ended.
The silence that followed was louder than any sound.
For three seconds, nothing moved. Nothing breathed.
Then the dining hall erupted.
Patients were on their feet. Some were cheering. Some were crying. Some were shouting questions at the staff, who had no answers.
Nurse Cross burst through the admin wing doors, her face a mask of fury. She saw Chloe standing in the hallway, phone in hand.
"You."
"Me."
"Do you have any idea what you have done?"
"Yes. I told the truth. It felt great."
Nurse Cross took a step toward her. For a moment, Chloe thought the woman might actually hit her.
But she stopped.
Because behind Chloe, the patients of Ward B had gathered.
Tiffany. Sarah. Denise. Mia. Amara.
They stood in a line, blocking the hallway.
"You will have to go through us," Tiffany said. Her voice was shaking. But she did not move.
Nurse Cross looked at the line of women. Then at Chloe.
"This is not over."
"No," Chloe agreed. "It is just beginning."
Dr. Webber appeared at the end of the hallway. His face was pale. His tie was crooked. He looked like a man who had just watched his world collapse and did not know how to stop it.
"The recording is already out," he said. "Someone sent it to the press before she played it over the intercom."
Chloe had not sent it to the press. But she knew who had.
Priya.
The lawyer.
The broadcast was not the climax. The broadcast was the distraction.
The real weapon was already in the hands of the journalists.
At 8:00 PM that night, Chloe sat in the courtyard with her crew.
Five women. One bench. One shared understanding.
"What happens now?" Tiffany asked.
"Now we wait," Chloe said. "The story is out. The evidence is with the lawyers. The journalists are working."
"And if nothing happens?"
"Something will happen. A place like this cannot survive exposure. It only survived because no one was looking."
She looked at the lights of the administration building.
"Now everyone is looking."
At midnight, the phones started ringing.
The facility's main line. Dr. Webber's personal line. The admin office.
Journalists. Dozens of them.
Chloe sat in her room and listened to the chaos through the thin walls. She could hear Webber shouting. Nurse Cross running. Orderlies scrambling.
The facility was coming apart.
Mia appeared in her doorway. "Priya sent a message. The story is running on three major news sites. They are calling it 'The Serenity Peak Scandal.'"
"Already?"
"It went viral. The recording, the financial documents, the patient files. Someone leaked everything."
Chloe looked at the camera in her room. For the first time, she did not feel watched. She felt seen.
"We are getting out of here," she said. "All of us."
"When?"
"Tomorrow. When the news vans arrive."
Mia's eyes widened. "You think they will send news vans?"
"I know they will. This story is too big to ignore."
She looked out her window at the dark road leading to the facility.
*Come on,* she thought. *Come get us.*
She leaned back on her bed and closed her eyes. The cameras were still watching. The doors were still locked. The facility was still standing.
But for the first time in twenty-nine days, Chloe felt something she had almost forgotten.
Hope.
